title: LB Broth Agar Plates with Antibiotics
steps: Add 125 mL of deionized water to a 250 mL Erlenmeyer Flask.
Add 3.125 g of LB Broth powder ( 25 g per 1 L of water) to the water in the Erlenmeyer Flask.
Add 1.875 g of agar to the water ( 1.5% of 125 mL ) in the Erlenmeyer Flask.
Cover the opening of the Erlenmeyer Flask with aluminum foil.
Place a 10 mm strip of autoclave tape on the aluminum covering the opening of the flask.

Autoclave. Electronic Table-Top Autoclave (EZ11Plus, Tuttnauer, MAN205-0443001EN Rev S, https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/gpm2bqtnp.jpeg , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/gpmzbqtnp.pdf )
After autoclaving, place the flask in the water bath at 60°C for 00:15:00 . General Purpose Digital Water Bath (PolyScience, WBE20A11B, https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/gpfhbqtnp.jpeg )
Add 1 µL of the antibiotic stock solution per mL of LB agar in the flask.
Stir on the stirring plate for 00:01:00 .
Pipette 5 mL of the LB agar mix into 20 60 mm petri dishes.
Leave the surface of the agar 1/4 uncovered in the laminar flow hood for 00:30:00 .
creator: Bec18
